title: The Security Council fails to adopt a ceasefire resolution in Gaza due to an American veto
text:
The UN Security‚Äã Council, at its‚Å¢ session held today, Wednesday, failed ‚Äçto adopt a ‚Äçresolution for ‚Å£a cease-fire in Gaza due to an American veto.
For more news, subscribe to our channel on‚Å¢ Telegram All members of‚Å£ the Security Council voted ‚Å¢in favor of the‚Äç draft‚Äå resolution recommending a cease-fire‚Å§ in Gaza, except the‚Å§ United‚Äã States.
The draft resolution calls on all‚Äç parties to comply with their‚Äã responsibilities in accordance with international law and to provide basic services and humanitarian aid to the residents of the Gaza Strip, in addition ‚Äçto the commitment of the‚Å£ parties‚Å£ to international law and international humanitarian law, particularly in relation to to ‚Äåprotect civilians and their civilian property.
What are the potential consequences ‚Äåof the UN Security Council ‚Äòs inability to pass a resolution for a cease-fire in Gaza?‚Äå Interview with Dr. Jane Melrose, International Relations Expert on UN Security Council‚Äôs Failed Resolution for Gaza Cease-Fire
Time.news Editor (TNE): Thank you for joining us today,‚Äã Dr. Melrose. Can you explain the significance of this veto in the context of international relations?

The American veto is ‚Äça significant event, primarily because it ‚Äådemonstrates the United States‚Äô position on the ongoing conflict in Gaza. By opposing‚Äç the resolution that called for a cease-fire and‚Å§ the adherence to‚Å¢ international law, ‚Å§the U.S. is signaling its continued support for Israel amidst the violence. This creates a complex dynamic,‚Å£ as it not only affects regional stability but also international diplomatic‚Äå relations, particularly with countries advocating for humanitarian considerations in the conflict.
TNE: ‚Å¢ The draft resolution garnered support from all other Security Council members. What does ‚Å§this disparity in voting say about global ‚Äãperspectives‚Äç on‚Å£ the conflict?
J.M.: The unanimous support for the ‚Å§resolution among all other Council members highlights a growing concern for humanitarian issues in conflict zones. The U.S.‚Äôs solitary position may cause friction with its traditional allies, impacting‚Å£ its influence on international forums.

J.M.: Failing to adopt the resolution has grave implications for both Gaza residents and the credibility of international law. It raises questions about accountability for violations against civilians and can embolden parties to continue aggressive actions with less fear of repercussions. Furthermore, it sets a concerning precedent for how international law may ‚Äåbe regarded in future conflicts, potentially leading to further humanitarian crises.
TNE: ‚Å¢Looking forward, what steps can the international community take to address the situation in Gaza?
J.M.: The international community must engage in active diplomacy and humanitarian outreach. J.M.: First, it‚Äôs important for readers to stay informed and critically engage with reputable news ‚Å¢sources.
